heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 225 on 28 May 2026, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 27 May 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 1 heatwavess, with an average of 1.8 per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (232°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.8°C), outside temperature (down 1.8°C), and inside temperature (down 1.5°C) and drier conditions in outside humidity (down 5%).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 5.5°C), feels like temperature (up 7.7°C), outside temperature (up 4.9°C), and inside temperature (up 1.9°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 664.2m higher than yesterday, 319.7m higher than the past week, 1135.7m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1381m minimum and 5470m maximum, with an average of 3740m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 3420m and 2600m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will remain comfortably hot. Outside; it will generally feel hot. Also, humidity should not be a concern and near calm conditions. Drizzle or light rain possible, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and very bright daylight. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
